key_insight: The paper adds computable two-sided a posteriori error certificates for PINNs solving ODEs, combining new lower bounds with localized upper bounds so the approximation error can be bracketed without knowing the exact solution.
engineering_relevance: This gives practitioners a more reliable way to trust PINN predictions in scientific computing, especially when deploying models for safety- or certification-sensitive dynamical systems.
method: Derive lower error bounds under a localized strong monotonicity condition on certified state-space domains.
Pair these with upper bounds using a one-sided Lipschitz condition, avoiding reliance on a global Lipschitz constant.
Provide explicit linear-system formulas via eigenvalues of the symmetric part, plus a signed-residual finite-probe certificate when initial conditions are enforced exactly.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical derivations and explicit formulas for ODEs and linear time-invariant/time-varying systems, rather than a numerical benchmark study.
watch_item: The certificates require verifiable local monotonicity/growth assumptions and may become uninformative when initial conditions are hard-enforced or when the certified domain is not suitable.
paper_title: Reliable Error Estimation for PINNs: Lower and Upper A Posteriori Bounds
